Which department offers the most number of degrees? List department name and id.

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT department_name, department_id
FROM Departments
JOIN Degree_Programs ON Departments.department_id = Degree_Programs.department_id
GROUP BY department_id
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;
reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_oneshot:
SELECT d.department_name, d.department_id
FROM Departments d
JOIN Degree_Programs dp ON d.department_id = dp.department_id
GROUP BY d.department_id
ORDER BY COUNT(dp.degree_program_id) DESC
LIMIT 1;

final_sql:
SELECT d.department_name, d.department_id
FROM Departments d
JOIN Degree_Programs dp ON d.department_id = dp.department_id
GROUP BY d.department_id
ORDER BY COUNT(dp.degree_program_id) DESC
LIMIT 1;

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT course_name, course_id
FROM Courses
WHERE course_id IN (
    SELECT course_id
    FROM Sections
    GROUP BY course_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) <= 2
);

GROUP BY c.course_id

final_sql:

FROM Courses
WHERE course_id IN (
    SELECT course_id
    FROM Sections
    GROUP BY course_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) <= 2
);

question: For each semester, what is the name and id of the one with the most students registered?

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT s.semester_name, se.student_id, s.first_name, s.last_name
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Students s ON se.student_id = s.student_id
WHERE (s.semester_id, se.student_id) IN (
    SELECT semester_id, student_id
    FROM Student_Enrolment
    GROUP BY semester_id, student_id
    ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
    LIMIT 1
);
reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_oneshot:
SELECT s.semester_name, se.student_id, s.first_name, s.last_name
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Students s ON se.student_id = s.student_id
GROUP BY s.semester_id
ORDER BY COUNT(se.student_id) DESC
LIMIT 1;

final_sql:
SELECT s.semester_name, se.student_id, s.first_name, s.last_name
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Students s ON se.student_id = s.student_id
WHERE (s.semester_id, se.student_id) IN (
    SELECT semester_id, student_id
    FROM Student_Enrolment
    GROUP BY semester_id, student_id
    ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
    LIMIT 1
);

question: Which student has enrolled for the most times in any program? List the id, first name, middle name, last name, the number of enrollments and student id.

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT 
    s.student_id, 
    s.first_name, 
    s.middle_name, 
    s.last_name, 
    COUNT(*) AS enrollment_count
FROM 
    Students s
JOIN 
    Student_Enrolment se ON s.student_id = se.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.student_id, s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name
ORDER BY 
    enrollment_count DESC
LIMIT 1;
reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_oneshot:
SELECT 
    s.student_id, 
    s.first_name, 
    s.middle_name, 
    s.last_name, 
    COUNT(se.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count
FROM 
    Students s
JOIN 
    Student_Enrolment se ON s.student_id = se.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.student_id
ORDER BY 
    enrollment_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

final_sql:
SELECT 
    s.student_id, 
    s.first_name, 
    s.middle_name, 
    s.last_name, 
    COUNT(*) AS enrollment_count
FROM 
    Students s
JOIN 
    Student_Enrolment se ON s.student_id = se.student_id
GROUP BY 
    s.student_id, s.first_name, s.middle_name, s.last_name
ORDER BY 
    enrollment_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Find the last name of the students who currently live in the state of North Carolina but have not registered in any degree program.

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT DISTINCT s.last_name
FROM Students s
JOIN Addresses a ON s.current_address_id = a.address_id
WHERE a.state_province_county = 'North Carolina'
AND s.student_id NOT IN (
    SELECT se.student_id
    FROM Student_Enrolment se
);

    WHERE state_province_county = 'North Carolina'

AND student_id NOT IN (
    SELECT student_id
    FROM Student_Enrolment
);

question: Show the date and id of the transcript with at least 2 course results.

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT T.transcript_date, T.transcript_id
FROM Transcripts T
JOIN Transcript_Contents TC ON T.transcript_id = TC.transcript_id
GROUP BY T.transcript_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2;
reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_oneshot:
SELECT T.transcript_date, T.transcript_id
FROM Transcripts T
JOIN Transcript_Contents TC ON T.transcript_id = TC.transcript_id
GROUP BY T.transcript_id
HAVING COUNT(TC.student_course_id) >= 2;

final_sql:
SELECT T.transcript_date, T.transcript_id
FROM Transcripts T
JOIN Transcript_Contents TC ON T.transcript_id = TC.transcript_id
GROUP BY T.transcript_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2;

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_oneshot:
SELECT T.transcript_date, T.transcript_id
FROM Transcripts T
JOIN Transcript_Contents TC ON T.transcript_id = TC.transcript_id
GROUP BY T.transcript_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT TC.student_course_id) >= 2;

final_sql:
SELECT T.transcript_date, T.transcript_id
FROM Transcripts T
JOIN Transcript_Contents TC ON T.transcript_id = TC.transcript_id
GROUP BY T.transcript_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT TC.student_course_id) >= 2;

Which address holds the most number of students currently? List the address id and all lines.

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2, a.line_3
FROM Addresses a

WHERE s.date_left IS NULL
GROUP BY a.address_id
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;
reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_oneshot:
SELECT a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2, a.line_3
FROM Addresses a
JOIN Students s ON a.address_id = s.current_address_id
WHERE s.date_left IS NULL
GROUP BY a.address_id
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;

final_sql:

question: What is the id, line 1, and line 2 of the address with the most students?

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2
FROM Addresses a
JOIN Students s ON a.address_id = s.current_address_id
GROUP BY a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1;
reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_oneshot:
SELECT a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2
FROM Addresses a
JOIN Students s ON a.address_id = s.current_address_id
GROUP BY a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2
ORDER BY COUNT(s.student_id) DESC
LIMIT 1;

final_sql:
SELECT a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2
FROM Addresses a
JOIN Students s ON a.address_id = s.current_address_id
GROUP BY a.address_id, a.line_1, a.line_2
ORDER BY COUNT(s.student_id) DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Find the semester when both Master students and Bachelor students got enrolled in.

reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_contract:
SELECT semester_id
FROM Student_Enrolment
WHERE degree_program_id IN (
    SELECT degree_program_id
    FROM Degree_Programs
    WHERE degree_summary_name LIKE '%Master%'
)
INTERSECT
SELECT semester_id
FROM Student_Enrolment
WHERE degree_program_id IN (
    SELECT degree_program_id
    FROM Degree_Programs
    WHERE degree_summary_name LIKE '%Bachelor%'
);
reasoner_decision.candidate_sql_oneshot:
SELECT s.semester_name
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Degree_Programs dp ON se.degree_program_id = dp.degree_program_id
WHERE dp.degree_summary_name LIKE '%Master%'
INTERSECT
SELECT s.semester_name
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Degree_Programs dp ON se.degree_program_id = dp.degree_program_id
WHERE dp.degree_summary_name LIKE '%Bachelor%';

final_sql:
SELECT s.semester_name
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Degree_Programs dp ON se.degree_program_id = dp.degree_program_id
WHERE dp.degree_summary_name LIKE '%Master%'
INTERSECT
SELECT s.semester_name
FROM Semesters s
JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.semester_id = se.semester_id
JOIN Degree_Programs dp ON se.degree_program_id = dp.degree_program_id
WHERE dp.degree_summary_name LIKE '%Bachelor%';
